title: IPMC SARS-CoV-2 One-Step qPCR Protocol on BIOMARK
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1dhqp","text":"- This protocol has been validated using an in vitro transcribed RNA positive control (from a  plasmid: 2019-nCoV_N_Positive Control, ref 10006625, IDT) and clinical transport media from SARS-CoV-2 diagnosed-patients.    ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":25,"length":61},{"style":"bold","offset":157,"length":59},{"style":"italic","offset":44,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7rn41","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ee5m","text":"The assay has been set up using the CDC’s recommended set of primers / probes (CDC 2019-nCoV primer/probe set: N1, N2  and a human RNP control). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":36,"length":109}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"clg03","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2esk1","text":"Titration of the fully synthetic SARS-CoV-2 RNA control shows a sensitivity of around 7 copies. Dilution assay of an RNA sample from a strong positive patient indicates a limit at around 20 CT at a dilution of 106.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":17,"length":39},{"style":"bold","offset":86,"length":10},{"style":"sup","offset":212,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b5t5k","text":"  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4hsag","text":"We have validated additional set of primers / probes and we recommend the use of the 3 SARS-CoV-2 probes and a human RNP control presented in the table, below. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":84,"length":2},{"style":"bold","offset":87,"length":72}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7rg6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dckop","text":"- The protocol is based on the use of the 192.24 IFC. However, it can also be used on all Biomark IFC and can be adapted for anay number of primer / probe sets and samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":42,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ee1vf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"do0jb","text":"- Regarding the extraction step, we have opted for the QIAamp Viral RNA Mini Kit. We have also validated a degraded solution using the miRNeasy Serum / Plasma Advanced Kit (Qiagen) due to the unavailability of the QIAamp DSP Viral RNA Mini Kit at the beginning of the project. One optional step has been added to the miRNeasy Serum / Plasma Advanced Kit, using yeast t-RNA as a carrier RNA. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":16,"length":15},{"style":"bold","offset":54,"length":28},{"style":"bold","offset":135,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3cijt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5gcad","text":"- Since our probes are designed with 5' 6-FAM/3'BHQ-1 chemistry, we have integrated this parameter in the biomark software for the detection. The parameter \"FAM/MGB\" also looks compatible for detection of these probes according to Fluidigm. steps: To be performed in the appropriate biosafety conditions (BSL2 laboratory) Transfer the totality of the transport medium into a 2 mL cryotube and stored at -80°C in a hermetically sealed body bag. If performing RNA extraction prior to RT-PCR, proceed to steps 2 to 8. If performing RT-PCR on samples without RNA extraction, go directly to step 9.
Add 560 Buffer AVL. Close the tube caps and vortex for >15 s. Note: If using a volume of sample other than 140 µL, increase the amount of Buffer AVL proportionally.
lncubate for 10 min Room temperature
Briefly centrifuge the tube to remove drops from the inside of the lid
Tubes can be thouroughly wiped with PHAGO’SPRAY ND (Phagogene) or Virospray (Sterisciences) and taken out the BSL2 laboratory.
Go to Qiacube. Elution volume: default 60 µL of AVE buffer. See Qiacube protocol in the attached file. Virus_QIAampViralRNA_BodyFluid_ManualLysis_V2.pdf
Extracted samples can be stored at -20 °C (or -80 °C) for further processing. Proceed to step 10.

Pool primer and probes : - Intermediate solution : mix together primers and probe for each target gene ( substep 10.1) - Pooled Taqman assay mix : pool intermediate solution (each assay) into a final pooled assay (substep 10.2)

Prepare One-step Reverse transcription and pre-amplification Pre-Mix : Components are from the Cells Direct One-Step qRT-PCR kit (Invitrogen, cat. no. 46-7200). In a DNA-free hood, prepare the following premix: %s %s * Includes 10% overage.
In a PCR plate, aliquot 19.5 μL of pre-mix for each sample.

Remove the plate from the DNA-free hood and add 5.5 μL of sample to each well containing pre-mix, making a total volume of 25 μL. If raw sample if used, this step should be performed in a BSL2 laboratory.
Mix the reactions by briefly vortexing, and then centrifuge 1000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute
After cycling, dilute the reaction 1:5 by adding 100 μL Dilution Reagent (Fluidigm PN 100-8726) to the 25 μL reaction volume for a total volume of 125 μL.

Manufacturer's instructions for general 192.24 IFC protocol 192.24_GE_TaqMan-Std_qr_100-6170.pdf Prepare the IFC Inject control line fluid into accumulator 2 (Acc2) on the IFC. Manufacturer's intruction for control line fluid loading Control+Line+Fluid_Loading_qr_68000132r07.pdf
Remove and discard the blue protective film from the bottom of the IFC.
Prepare 10X Assays In a DNA-free hood, prepare aliquots of 10X assays using volumes in the following table. Scale up appropriately for multiple runs. %s %s
Prepare Sample Pre-Mix and Samples Combine components in the following table to make sample pre-mix and final sample mixture. Scale up appropriately for multiple runs. %s %s

In a DNA-free hood, combine the TaqMan Universal PCR Master Mix with the GE Sample Loading Reagent in a 1.5 mL sterile tube—enough volume to fill an entire IFC. 2.2 μL of this sample pre-mix can then be aliquoted for each sample.
Remove these aliquots from the DNA-free hood and add 1.8 μL of cDNA to each, making a total volume of 4 μL in each aliquot.
Pipet 3 μL of each assay and 3 μL of each sample into the respective inlets on the IFC (see the 192.24 IFC pipetting map).
Pipet 150 μL of Actuation Fluid into the P1 well on the IFC.
Pipet 150 μL of Pressure Fluid into the P2 and P3 wells on the IFC.
Pipet 20 μL of Pressure Fluid into the P4 and P5 wells on the IFC.
Blot the carrier surface with a dry, lint-free cloth.
Start data collection on Biomark HD Biomark HD (Fluidigm, BMKHD-BMKHD)
Click Start a New Run. Ensure that the status indicators for the lamp and the camera are green.
Place the loaded IFC into the Biomark HD.
Choose project settings (if applicable). Click Next.
Click Load.
Verify IFC barcode and IFC type.
Choose project settings (if applicable). Click Next.
Provide a name and select a file storage location for a new IFC run, or browse to select a predefined run file. Click Next.
Choose the application, reference, and probes: a Application type: Gene Expression b Passive reference: ROX c Assay: Single probe d Probe type: FAM-Non fluo or FAM-MGB e Click Next.
Browse to and choose the thermal protocol: below the thermal protocol (Taqman Covid) we have used:
Confirm Auto Exposure is selected. Click Next.
Verify IFC run information
Click Start Run
See manufacturer's instructions in the following file. HB-0354-007_HB_QA_Viral_RNA_Mini_0720_WW.pdf Transfer 140 µL sample into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

If performing RT-qPCR without prior RNA extraction and purification, starting samples in Viral Transport Medium or in 10mM Tris-HCl pH 6.8 and 1mM EDTA, proceed to either of the following: - heat inactivate only (substep 9.1). - treat with QuickExtract DNA extraction DNA solution (from Lucigen) or 0,5% Tween 20 and 2mg/mL Proteinase K (substeps 9.2 and 9.3).

If performing direct heat inactivation only: Transfer 25µl of sample in a 0.2 mL PCR-grade tube and incubate 5 min at 95°C in a thermo cycler. Then, keep the treated sample at +4°C until the One Step RT-PreAmp reaction (proceed to Step 10).

If performing treatment using QuickExtract DNA extraction solution or Tween 20/Proteinase K: Prepare a 2X master mix solution for the Tween 20/Proteinase K treatment (using 2mg/mL proteinase K in 0,5% tween 20 as final conditions), as indicated in the following table: %s %s In 0.2mL PCR-grade tubes, combine 25 µL of the 2X Tween 20/ProteinaseK master mix or 25 µL of QuickExtract DNA extraction solution with 2 5µL of the sample. Pipet up and down to mix well and spin down quickly.

Incubate the tube at 65°C for 10 min, 95°C for 5 min. Do not incubate tubes directly at +4°C but instead let the treated samples at room temperature (optional: you can store tube on ice afterwards). Samples are ready to use, proceed to step 10.
Prepare and intermediate solution by mixing primers and probe for each target, with the following volumes: %s %s

Pool each intermediate solution into a pooled Taqman assay mix (0,2X) using TE (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 0.1 mM EDTA) so that each primer is at a final concentration of 180 nM, and each probe at 50 nM. This step should be adapted depending on the number of primers/probes to be used in the final pooled assay (ideally 8 or 12 sets of primers for a 192.24 IFC). The chart below provides an example using 4 assays used to detect SARS-CoV-2 %s %s Note: Volume can be adjusted proportionally based on the number of samples to be amplified
Place in a standard thermal cycler. Incubate using the following protocol: %s %s
Verify IFC barcode and IFC type.
Choose project settings (if applicable). Click Next.
Provide a name and select a file storage location for a new IFC run, or browse to select a predefined run file. Click Next.

Choose the application, reference, and probes: a) Application type: Gene Expression b) Passive reference: ROX c) Assay: Single probe d) Probe type: if using a dark quencher choose FAM-Non fluo (Need to be added manually into the Biomark) or FAM-MGB . e) Click Next.
Browse to and choose the thermal protocol: below the thermal protocol (Taqman Covid) we have used:
Confirm Auto Exposure is selected. Click Next.
Verify IFC run information
Click Start Run
Prime IFC on the Controller. Place the IFC into the instrument and run the load script: - Juno:Load Mix 192.24 GE - RX: Load Mix (169x) IMPORTANT : Start IFC run within 1 hour of loading samples.
Remove any dust particles or debris from the IFC surface with clear tape.
Click Load.
Choose project settings (if applicable). Click Next.
Place the loaded IFC into the Biomark HD.
Click Start a New Run. Ensure that the status indicators for the lamp and the camera are green.
Double-click the Data Collection icon on the desktop.
Start data collection on Biomark HD Biomark HD (Fluidigm, BMKHD-BMKHD)
creator: Laure-Emmanuelle Zaragosi
